title: Blood Relations

glossary.definition: Related through the mother side of the family.
The starting person you are using to map out a relationship.
Levels in a family tree, like grandparents, parents, and children.

hook.scenario: Every TCS/Infosys reasoning section throws a 'Pointing to a photo, X said...' puzzle.
hook.question: Do you solve it in your head and second-guess, or in 15 seconds on paper?

mental_model.intuition: Don't track relationships in words - draw the family TREE.
mental_model.description: Generations stack top-to-bottom; a married couple sits side by side. Once it's a diagram, the answer is just reading the path between two boxes - no working memory required.

method: Spot the two people and the link word (son, sister, maternal uncle, father-in-law).
Anchor the speaker / reference person as a box; draw generations top (elder) to bottom (younger).
Mark gender (+ for male, - for female) and couples (=), parent-child with a vertical line.
Read the path between the two target boxes to name the relation.

worked_example.problem: Pointing at a man, Riya says: 'He is the son of my grandfather's only son.' How is the man related to Riya?
steps: 'Grandfather's only son' = Riya's father (he is the single son of the grandfather).
The man is the son of Riya's father.
Son of Riya's father = Riya's brother.

shortcuts.title: Collapse 'only' phrases
shortcuts.trick: 'Only son / only daughter of X' usually collapses to one known person - replace it immediately.
shortcuts.example: 'My mother's only son' = me (if male) or my brother.

common_mistakes.title: Assuming gender from the name or context
common_mistakes.explanation: 'The child of X' may have unstated gender. Only use the gender the puzzle explicitly gives - guessing flips the answer.

common_mistakes.title: Reading the chain in words instead of drawing it
common_mistakes.explanation: Multi-hop relations like 'father-in-law of the brother of...' exceed working memory. Draw the tree and the error rate drops to near zero.

recall_questions.q: What is the first move on any blood-relations puzzle?
recall_questions.answer: Draw the family tree - anchor the reference person and stack generations top to bottom.

recall_questions.q: How do you simplify 'my grandfather's only son'?
recall_questions.answer: 'Only son' of the grandfather collapses to the speaker's father - replace the phrase with one box.

recall_questions.q: Why is solving these in your head so error-prone?
recall_questions.answer: Multi-hop relations exceed working memory; the diagram externalizes them so you just read a path between two boxes.

oa_questions.question: A is B's sister. C is B's mother. D is C's father. How is A related to D?
oa_questions.approach: Tree: D -> C -> (A, B). A is female (sister of B) and sits two generations below D, so A is D's granddaughter.

oa_questions.question: Pointing to a woman, Karan said, 'She is the daughter of the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to Karan?
oa_questions.approach: 'Only daughter of my mother' = Karan's sister. The woman is that sister's daughter = Karan's niece.
